rag_rules: Place input envelope assumptions in component type annexes when they refer to component ports and use visible feature names.
Use valid AGREE syntax for assumptions and guarantees, ensuring type compatibility and scope alignment with AADL model structure.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid AADL component type or implementation scopes based on the nature of the contract.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference only visible identifiers and types present in the AADL model.
Constants and equations in AGREE should use types and names that are explicitly declared or visible in the model scope.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers or types that are not part of the analyzed AADL model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` and must follow valid AGREE syntax for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations.
Assumptions and guarantees should use valid AADL feature names and types; avoid referencing non-existent or non-visible identifiers.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes unless they are represented through valid variables or outputs.
Do not assume numeric types or ranges for Boolean or event ports unless explicitly defined in the AADL model.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations such as Compute_Execution_Time, Deadline, Period, Priority, Dispatch_Protocol, Stack_Size, Code_Size, or unit tokens like ms/us/Hz/Bytes
Event port numeric encoding or 0/1 equality over Boolean or opaque data ports without explicit model support
